summary: Thorough implementation-resolved blueprint for a VS Code-style file browser dashboard feature. Excellent causal coverage of user interactions, security boundaries, and degradation behavior via when-then chains. Weakened by enumerative entity catalogs (38+ named entities across Browser, Api, Ui) where many are defined for containment but never referenced in behavioral rules, diluting semantic density below Agentish expectations. Scoping blocks and decision records are well-structured and actionable.
strengths: When-then chains comprehensively cover user interactions, error degradation, auth enforcement, mutation guardrails, and watch lifecycle — high causal clarity for an implementation-resolved file
V1 scope is explicitly bounded with clear future-readiness markers (multiRootReady, safeMutations) that prevent scope creep without foreclosing evolution
Security concerns (path traversal, symlink escape, session tokens in URLs) are addressed inline within behavioral rules rather than deferred to a separate security document
Package decisions, route names, and usesFiles listing make this directly actionable for an implementing agent without ambiguity about where code lives
Enforces block is precise and non-redundant — each rule constrains a distinct aspect of the feature boundary
weaknesses: Entity catalog is enumerative: ~38 named entities across Browser, Api, Ui, Runtime objects, but several (clipboardIntent, revealTarget, metadata, filterInput) appear only in definitions or containment — never in when-then rules — creating catalog bulk without proportional semantic yield

Containment declarations (Browser.backend.contains, Dashboard.screen.contains) restate topology that is already recoverable from the entity grouping objects, adding token cost for low marginal information

Decision blocks vary in density: featureId and routeName are thin (feature id should be files) while watchStrategy and mutationScope carry real tradeoff reasoning — the thin ones could fold into enforces
No explicit reference to or dependency on a product-ideal companion blueprint; for an implementation_resolved file this makes it harder to verify it faithfully resolves a higher-layer specification
